text: Handling deadlines is a key skill for both work and study. Good time management involves breaking a project into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each one. Prioritization means identifying which tasks are most important or urgent, so you can focus on them first. You should also practice scope management by saying "no" to extra requests when your plate is full. Finally, communication is vital: if you see a deadline slipping, inform your team or manager early. This allows everyone to adjust expectations or offer help. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines without unnecessary stress.
